statement.markdown: Given a string containing a sequence of characters separated by spaces, implement a function to determine if the string represents a valid queue. A valid queue is a sequence of characters that can be dequeued from front to back and rear to front without violating the queue property (i.e., the first character of the string is the first character of the queue, and the last character of the string is the last character of the queue).
statement.inputFormat: A string containing a sequence of characters separated by spaces
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether the string represents a valid queue
statement.notes: Time complexity: O(n), where n is the number of characters in the string. Space complexity: O(n), where n is the number of characters in the string.

hints.text: Think about how you can convert the string into a valid queue

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: bool isValidQueue(string s) { /* TODO: implement */ } };
starterCode.java: public class Solution { public boolean isValidQueue(String s) { /* TODO: implement */ } }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def isValidQueue(self, s: str) -> bool: pass

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: bool isValidQueue(string s) { int n = s.size(); for (int i = 1; i < n; i += 2) { if (s[i] < s[i-1]) return false; } return true; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public boolean isValidQueue(String s) { int n = s.length(); for (int i = 1; i < n; i += 2) { if (s.charAt(i) < s.charAt(i-1)) return false; } return true; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def isValidQueue(self, s: str) -> bool: n = len(s); for i in range(1, n, 2): if s[i] < s[i-1]: return False; return True
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function isValidQueue(s) { var n = s.length; for (var i = 1; i < n; i += 2) { if (s[i] < s[i-1]) return false; } return true; }

solutions.python: def isValidQueue(s: str) -> bool: n = len(s); q = []; for c in s.split(): q.append(int(c)); for i in range(n // 2): if q[i] > q[n - i - 1]: return False; return True
solutions.cpp: #include <iostream> using namespace std; bool isValidQueue(string s) { int n = s.size(); int q[n]; for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) cin >> q[i]; for (int i = 0; i < n / 2; i++) if (q[i] > q[n - 1 - i]) return false; return true; }
